And in this case, we are talking about one of the best cameras on the market. This category undoubtedly goes to the iPhone, and in terms of battery life, this iPhone has 4,000 mAh. Well, first of all, it depends on whether you prefer iOS or Android. title: Fix Wi-Fi No Internet Connection on iPhone 17 Pro, 17 Pro Max
transcript: Here's how to fix Wi-Fi issues when your iPhone says it's connected but shows no internet connection. The first basic solution is to forget and reconnect to Wi-Fi. Open the settings app on your iPhone. Tap Wi-Fi. If it says no internet connection, tap the info icon next to your Wi-Fi network. Then choose forget this network. Tap forget again to confirm. After that, reconnect to your Wi-Fi network and check if it works. The second fix is to turn airplane mode on and off. Swipe down from the top right corner of your screen to open the control center. Tap the airplane mode icon. Wait a few seconds, then turn it back off. The third solution is to restart your iPhone and router. Quickly press and release the volume up button, then the volume down button, and press and hold the side button until you see the Apple logo. Release the button and let your iPhone restart. To restart the router, unplug it from power, wait 30 seconds, then plug it back in. Wait for it to fully boot up and then try connecting your iPhone again. Now, the fourth solution, reset network settings. If the problem keeps happening, you'll need to reset your network settings. Open the settings app. Scroll down and tap general. Then scroll to the bottom and tap transfer or reset iPhone. Tap reset. Then choose reset network settings. Enter your passcode and confirm. Wait for your iPhone to restart. Then unlock it and reconnect to your Wi-Fi network. Sometimes network issues come from pending software updates. So, here's the fifth solution. Check and update your software version. Open the settings app. Go to general and tap software update. Wait while your iPhone checks for updates. If it says your software is up to date, skip this step. But if an update's available, tap download and install. Once the updates done, check if your Wi-Fi connection works normally. The sixth solution is to enable Wi-Fi assist. Wi-Fi assist is a handy feature that keeps your internet connection stable. When your Wi-Fi signal gets weak, it automatically switches to cellular data. To turn it on, open the settings app, tap cellular, then scroll down and turn on the switch next to Wi-Fi assist. If you don't want to use mobile data, you can turn it off instead. Finally, try another network. If you're still having trouble, connect to a different Wi-Fi network to see if the internet works there. For more detailed solutions, tap the info button at the top right corner to watch the full video on fixing Wi-Fi issues on iPhone. If nothing helps, contact your internet service provider, ISP, for a deeper check. They can test your modem, router, and account for possible issues or signal problems. If everything looks fine on their end, reach out to Apple support. Open Safari on your iPhone. Visit the Apple support website and sign in with your Apple ID. Select your device, describe your problem, and follow the onscreen steps. You can also book a Genius Bar appointment and visit a nearby Apple store for repair if needed. Hope this video helped. Don't forget to like, share, and subscribe for more iPhone tips and tricks.

title: HONEYMOON is OVER - iPhone 17 Pro Max 30 Days LATER

title: I Tried Apple’s New TechWoven Case with the iPhone 17 Pro Deep Blue - Here’s the Truth
transcript: Okay, so I've been using the iPhone 7 Pro for a few weeks now and Apple just released these new cases called Tech Woven. The first thing I noticed right out of the box was how different it felt. Apple's been talking a lot about sustainability lately, and you can tell this case is a part of that story. When you touch it, it feels premium, not slippery like some of their older cases, and not sticky like the silicone either. It's somewhere in between with just enough grip that you don't feel like your phone's going to slide off the table every time you put it down. So, the Tech Movement cases come in five colors: black, green, purple, sienna, and blue. And Apple, for once, didn't make the colors too safe. The black is classic. It'll match literally everything. The green has a subtle earthy tone, kind of like olive with a little gray. The purple one's surprisingly elegant. It's not bright. It's more muted, kind of lavender meets graphite. Sienna, like I mentioned, feels like a fall in a color. And the blue honestly might be my favorite. Is rich and calm without being too loud. Okay, let's talk about fit because if you've ever bought a random third party case, you know how bad a stop if it can feel. It snaps onto the iPhone 17 Pro like it was made for it, which obviously it was. The edges line up cleanly with the buttons. The camera cutout is perfectly centered and everything feels secure. It's slightly thicker than Apple's old fine woven case, but in a good way. It feels more protective and solid. And it doesn't add much weight, and it still slides easily into a pocket or a backpack. The texture helps with grip, but what I love most is that it doesn't collect fingerprints like silicone cases. If you've ever had one of those cases that just look dirty after two days, yeah, it's not that. After using it for a week, I realized something. The best cases are the ones you stop thinking about. And that's kind of what this one became for me. It just works. It protects the phone without feeling bulky, and it looks good without screaming for attention. The buttons feel tactile, which is something that's always hit or miss on Apple cases. Here, the buttons are slightly metallic and clicky, and you cannot stop pressing them. Of course, it has full Maxafe compatibility. That little magnetic circle on the back locks perfectly with my charger and wallet. I've been using it on my Macsafe stand every night and had zero issues with alignment or slipping. So, the big question, how does it hold up? Apple's last fabric style case, the fine wo was not great. It looked beautiful on day one, but after a week, it showed scratches, stained, and it just didn't age well for most users. The tech woven is way better. The texture feels tighter and more durable, like it could actually handle daily life. I threw it in in my backpack a few times, set it on rough tabletops, and even got a little bit of water on it once. It still looks basically new. You might see a bit of wear over time. It's still a soft material, but it feels more like a high-end fabric that will developed character, not fall apart. I think Apple finally learned from the feedback last year, and this feels like a genuine improvement. As I said, I've been using it for a week now, and it has protected my iPhone 17 Pro quite well, and the case itself shows hardly any, or rather, no signs of wear. It's perfect for the iPhone. There's a slight lip around the screen and camera bump, or on its back, the screen is protected and doesn't touch the surface. And because of the grippy fabric texture, I actually found myself dropping it less, which is kind of the point, right? So, it's not an Otterbox, but it's not flimsy either. It's a nice middle ground protection with style. One thing about Apple's marketing photos, the colors almost never look exactly like they do in person. So, if you're trying to decide which one to get, here's how I describe them in real life. The black is simple, timeless, minimal. If you don't like attention, this is it. The green looks way better in person. It's kind of like a forest green with a hint of gray. The purple surprisingly versatile. It looks sophisticated and not girly purple. Sienna, warm, cozy, a little bit vintage. think fall outfit vibes. And the blue, clean, slightly muted navy tone goes with basically everything. If I had to pick one to recommend, I'd say blue or green. They're unique, but still wearable year round. So, a few random observations that stood out over the week. The case is surprisingly easy to clean. If you get a fingerprint or a smudge, just wipe it with a dry cloth. It doesn't soak up dirt like older fabric cases. The texture feels nice when you're holding the phone during long calls or watching videos. It's not cold or slick. It looks more expensive than it actually is. I know Apple's accessories are never cheap, but this one actually kind of feels worth it. The camera ring doesn't get in the way at all. It's flush and doesn't reflect flash weirdly like some third party cases do. There are small details, but they add up. It's one of those products where the more you use it, the more you appreciate the design choices. So, is it worth it? At the end of the day, it's still a phone case, but I must honestly say that the price value proposition is not that great because of its $59 price tag. There are actually other thirdparty cases that cost maybe less than half of this, and they also fit perfectly and protect your iPhone very well while looking good. But here's the thing, the iPhone 7 Pro is one of the most premium phones out there, so it kind of deserves a case that matches the same level of design and quality. If you're the kind of person who likes things that look and feel good, you'll probably love the Tech Woven case. title: Hoy enfrentamos al iPhone 17 Pro Max vs el Xiaomi 17 Pro en nuestro campeonato y tú elegirás 👊🔥
transcript: We're pitting the iPhone 17 Pro Max against the Xiaomi 17 Pro in our championship, where you have the power to choose which is the best smartphone.  The winner will face the winner of the Galaxy S25 Ultra versus Honor Magic 7 Pro battle. But hey, here we have Apple's most powerful smartphone, the iPhone 17 Pro Max, which costs around $1,200.  And on the other side we have the very popular Xiaomi 17 Pro for around $900.  And you can see that besides the name they have a very similar design, but with the difference that this Xiaomi has a 2.66-inch screen on the back where the cameras are, so a very good way to start the battle.  In terms of battery capacity, the iPhone has approximately 5000 mAh in the EIM version, but the Xiaomi enters the ring relying on its 6300 mAh battery and 100W fast charging, in addition to 50W wireless charging— faster than the iPhone's 45W wired charging. Regarding speed and performance, the Xiaomi is equipped with the new Snapdragon 8 Elite 5th generation, while the iPhone features the A19 Pro. These two are likely the most powerful in this entire competition.  In terms of cameras, the Xiaomi delivers a triple blow thanks to its three 50-megapixel sensors, which, in combination with its rear screen, essentially turn those cameras into its selfie cameras.  But the iPhone fights back and counterattacks with its triple rear camera of 48 megapixels and a unique 18- megapixel selfie camera.  Now it's your turn to choose which one is the best.  We're going to leave a comment for each smartphone you should like, and the winner will be announced on November 21st. We'll see which one will make it to the...

title: Jak přetvořit starší iPhone na 17 Pro?
transcript: I found a way to turn an old 14 into an iPhone 17 Pro. Videos of people changing old iPhones into new ones have been circulating on Instagram for 17 reasons.  They change the color to orange with a sticker, add a Keramic Shield to the phone, and of course, there's a redesigned camera island.  So it's a real wilderness.  Would you go for it? I suspect that someone who does will definitely be found in the comments.

title: 아이폰17pro vs 폴드7 비교기
transcript: Today's video is a comparison of the iPhone 17 Pro and the Galaxy Fold 7 based on my experience using them so far .  I'll explain the overall user experience, including design, durability, display, performance, camera, battery, and even cellular issues, so if you're still considering purchasing a phone, today's video will be helpful. This is something I really wanted to mention while using the Fold 7.  The iPhone has an IP68 rating, and the Galaxy Fold 7 has an IP48 rating .  The number in front indicates the dust resistance level, and the number in back indicates the water resistance level .  This means that while both models have the same water resistance rating, there is a clear difference in their dust resistance rating .  Actually, I was a little hesitant to buy the Fold because of this part. Yes, this is the site where I work. Dust and dirt are always floating in the air.  If you use a fold in this situation, will the durability of the white paper be okay?  I couldn't help but think that it would break down quickly because a lot of dust and new particles would enter through the gap .  But I ended up going ahead with the purchase, and now, after 4 months, the white paper is starting to creak a little .  When I first bought it, I really liked the tension of the white paper, but after 4 months, I could n't feel it anymore.  For comparison, when I first bought it, the tension was really good right before the fold was fully opened .  Here, if you apply a little force, the structure will unfold straight out .  It really felt so chewy .  But the retention has completely disappeared.  To fully unfold it now, you just have to push it all the way to 180 degrees.   Even when I pushed it almost all the way, the tension didn't feel strong and the fold did n't unfold any further but just held that angle .  In short, it would be correct to say that it went from a sticky tension to a dry tension.  It is expected that the gap between the folds is the main cause of dust and bird droppings entering. And when I first unfolded the white matter, it felt like it unfolded smoothly without any problems from the folded state until it was fully unfolded, but now I feel a slight discomfort in the middle .  Was the situation I was using too harsh?  Ultimately, I think it's the limit of the IP 48 rating. For those who use it in a different, cleaner environment than mine, a dustproof level 4 rating seems to be sufficient, but if you use the Fold in a dusty environment like mine, with a lot of iron powder, I think insurance is a must. First, let me tell you what the issues are regarding cellular .  We're currently experiencing cellular issues with both the standard and Pro models of the iPhone 17 series, particularly data disconnections and slow response times when using LT. First, let me tell you about the times when I feel uncomfortable. For example, when I write a message and press send on KakaoTalk, there is a delay in sending it.  I wondered if this was the original behavior, so I tried sending a KakaoTalk message in the same LT mode on my Galaxy phone, and it was sent immediately .  It's frustrating because this doesn't happen once or twice, but happens every time I send a message .  Some people also experienced data interruptions or delays when watching YouTube . To make an accurate diagnosis, we conducted a ping test on a total of five devices .  To cut to the chase, all my other devices were fine, but the problem only occurred on my iPhone 17 .  If the ping is over 100ms, that is, over 0.1 or 1 second, it is a value that can be felt as a slight lag, but as soon as I performed the ping test, the value was canceled for an absurd reason.  The deviation is huge, going back and forth after immediately passing 700 The graph fluctuated so much that I wondered if this was right .  The maximum value was 1000 m, which is a very high value exceeding 1 second, and this is a value where the delay is definitely felt in actual use.  Even looking at the average, we can see a significant difference of 335% compared to the Galaxy or iPhone's 16% . This is a figure that can cause inconvenience in daily life, and we are actually experiencing that inconvenience right now For Galaxy 24, it shows an average value of around 77 .  And the Galaxy S25.  25 The overall average value was 77, which is a good level, and the Galaxy Fold 7, cellular maximum value 111, average value 73, are incredibly stable figures, and the graph is also gentle.  The iPhone 16 Pro has a ping of 79 on average and 146 at maximum, which is a level that is not noticeable in actual use .  Ultimately, based on ping tests, we can see that updating to iOS 26.1 did not improve cellular issues .   I think the camera performance of both iPhones and Galaxy phones has now leveled out.  However, I think there may be differences in which side expresses the details a little better or in the colors .  In terms of specs, all 17% of the lenses are equipped with 48 million pixels, while the Fold 7 has 12 million pixels in the super-wide-angle lens and 10 million pixels in the telephoto lens, showing lower pixels than the iPhone, but in the most important main camera, it shows a significant difference compared to the 17%.  However, the difference in photographic results is not determined by pixels alone.  Now, let's compare the most used main lenses .  This is a photo of a wooden pillar.  Both came out so well.  It shows great details and colors as if it was taken with a professional camera .  But for detailed expressions, the iPhone was on top.  There is definitely a difference in the expression of the texture of the tree when the sunlight hits it .  The 17% bark portion clearly expresses each grain, while the fold photo expresses the grain boundaries slightly blurred .  And if you look at the dark part opposite the sunlight, the shadow, it's 17% darker, but it clearly expresses the shape of the tree bark, whereas the Fold 7 gives the feeling that the iPhone's Debbie is drawn in a lump.  It was a really clear, blue sky that day, but while 17% captured the sky well, Fold's photo had washed out the color of the sky.  In the end, the main lens's pixel count is about four times higher than that of the Fold, but the photo results that I was satisfied with were on the 17% side. Next, let's take a look at some telephoto photos. This is a proud photo of our country's Taegeukgi on the top of a mountain .  In reality, it was quite far away . I zoomed in on it at about 10x. In 17% of the photos, the Taegeuk mark of the Taegeukgi is clearly expressed, and the shape of a monster is also formed next to it .  Also, the texture of the fabric blowing in the wind was expressed better than expected .  On the other hand, the Taegeukgi of the Fold 7 was a photo result with a completely blurred resolution.  This time, let's compare photos at 30x magnification. 17% of the photos had relatively clear exterior wall shapes and apartment lines, while the Fold 7 photos had blurriness to the point where it was difficult to discern the shapes Also, the quality of the telephoto photos in the three magnification ranges was slightly better in the iPhone 17 Pro.  I think this also has the advantage of a difference between three and four magnifications.  And I would also like to raise my hand for the ultra-wide lens, which is 17% .  When the same subject was photographed in the same location, the resulting photo was 17% clearer and sharper.  And if you compare the short videos, the colors themselves are completely different.  The iPhone video showed clearer and richer colors, while the Fold 7 showed a slightly tinted result. In the end, the saying that I use the iPhone because of the camera still holds true for me .  What are your experiences ?   The iPhone 17 Pro features a 6.3-inch OLED display with a pixel resolution of 26226 .  On the other hand, the Galaxy Fold's cover display has a 21:9 ratio with a resolution of 2520 x 1080 on a 6.5-inch 5-inch display, while the internal display has a vast display with a resolution of 2184 x 1968 on an 8-inch display.   Even if we simply compare, the iPhone has one display and the Fold has two, and this difference dramatically improves the Fold's usability .  First of all, the most satisfying part is that anyone can use my external display according to the situation .  When you normally use the Fold, you do n't have to unfold it as much as you might think.  Now that I've used Fold for a while, I know for sure.  This means that the cover display is mostly used as the main display .  For most apps, you'll find the external display sufficiently useful .  That's why we use most of the apps we use on a daily basis without opening them .  I think the reduced thickness and weight do a great job of providing this kind of usability.   On the other hand, when you unfold the fold, you get the expansive usability of the internal display that can only be felt with the fold, and you must unfold the fold to view the photo album .  This is really useful when checking private photos, of course, but also when sharing them with multiple people .  The convenience of having a large screen by default without having to pinch your fingers to enlarge the photo is truly different.  And at first I thought the video size was not as big as I thought because of the letter boxes that appear above and below when watching the video, but the slightly larger screen size was satisfactory for watching the video .  You can enjoy the experience of using both a form and a mini tablet in one device However, the display quality was superior on the iPhone, with a pixel density of 460 ppi .  If you want a clear and bright screen when watching videos, the iPhone is more advantageous.  The Galaxy Fold's cover display quality was slightly disappointing compared to the iPhone.  Although the brightness from the large screen of the internal display was satisfactory, I would give the iPhone's display a thumbs up in terms of quality In fact, I think it is no match for us domestically .  Even people who really like iPhones are really envious of Samsung Pay on the Galaxy .  Even if you go out without your wallet, there is absolutely no anxiety or hesitation.  Even though I live in the countryside, it's hard to find a place where Samsung Pay isn't accepted .  When you go out with your iPhone, you must always take your wallet with you.  The magic of the iPhone that makes it so easy to put it in your pocket that you end up needing a bag when you go out .  While it's true that the number of stores accepting Apple Pay, such as convenience stores and coffee shops, has increased, it's still not perfect for taking your iPhone out and about . I think you all know this is crucial, so I'll leave it at that .   The iPhone 17 Pro is equipped with the A19 Pro chip made with the 3n process, and the Galaxy Fold 7 is equipped with the Snapdragon 8 Elite 10 set, also made with the 3n process .  Comparing the Bench 6 tests, the showed a single-core score of 3,878 points and a multi-core score of 9,999 points It's the most powerful smartphone in existence.  Meanwhile, let's also look at the Geekbench test results for the Fold 7 .  It scored 2978 points for single core and 9517 points for multi-core .  Single-core performance is about 30% higher on the iPhone than on the Galaxy, and multi-core performance is about 5% better on the iPhone. This can create a more comfortable experience on the iPhone when playing games or using standalone apps .  Also, since the heat control that I will mention later was also effective, it is a fact that the iPhone still has a significant advantage in terms of performance. On the other hand, the Fold 7's multi-core shows relatively similar figures to the iPhone, so it can be seen as optimized for multitasking, which is the Fold 7's strength Looking at the heat generation, after 10 minutes of shooting 4K video at 60 frames per second, the rear temperature of the iPhone was around 31 degrees, while the rear temperature of the Galaxy Fold 7 was confirmed to be around 38 degrees shows that there is a large temperature difference in the heat control. And the difference between the two devices is that the iPhone 17 Pro has a large camera island with an aluminum unibody, so all parts are structured to dissipate heat .  This allows us to see that the heat is evenly distributed throughout the entire rhizome, while also providing a structure that is advantageous for heat dissipation .  On the other hand, in the case of the Galaxy Fold 7, extremely hot heat is concentrated locally on the right side of the camera.  The iPhone's heat management is amazing.  In previous generations, overheating was cited as a drawback, but the performance and heat generation of the iPhone 17 series have been greatly improved .  The iPhone is far superior in terms of chipset performance and heat dissipation to maintain the highest efficiency on a continuous basis, making it a good choice for those who primarily use their phones in performance-intensive environments such as high-end games Galaxy Fold 7 has a battery capacity of 4,400 mAh, while the iPhone 17 Pro has a battery capacity of 3,945 mAh.   The capacity difference between the two devices is about 455m, meaning the Fold 7's physical battery size is about 12% larger than the iPhone's.  Because of its larger physical size, the Fold 7's battery might seem to last longer, but in my experience, I noticed slight differences depending on the environment I was using it in .  Today, we will start with both devices in 80% battery mode and use them together all day long under the same conditions .  First, we filmed the video at 4K 60 frames for about an hour .  After an hour of warning, the 17% was at 53% and the Fold 7 was at 55%, both similar levels, but the Fold 7's battery was slightly higher.  In the end, the iPhone drained 27% of its battery in an hour, while the Fold 7 drained 25% .  And then I went right out .  It was to find out about the battery capacity consumed while exercising .  Today's exercise is hiking. At the entrance to the mountain, both the iPhone and Galaxy were put into hiking mode.  It was a 2 hour and 45 minute round trip course, and I took a lot of photos and videos along the way When I finally finished the hike and came down, the battery level was 27% for the Fold 7 and 29% for the Fold 17. Before starting the hike, the Fold 7's battery was 2% higher, but after completing the hike, it was actually 2% lower .  It's true that the iPhone's battery efficiency appears to be superior in exercise tracking environments .  However, since the difference was not that great, it could be considered a small number. After that, I came home and watched Netflix for about an hour and then played a game. When using the phones under the same conditions starting at 80%, the phone that eventually discharged first was the iPhone 17 Pro.  But when it was 17% discharged, the Fold 7 had just 1% battery left.  I was very surprised to see that the battery life was so similar that I wondered if it was possible to show such similar usage times .  Ultimately, we were able to confirm that the battery life of the Fold 7, which had a physical advantage, was not necessarily longer .  Now, let's take a look at the battery and its annual charging speed .  The Fold 7 is said to have a 25W charging speed, and the 17% charging speed is said to be around 40W. Apple actually explains that using an adapter rated at 40W or higher will complete 50% battery charging. So I prepared generously. The charger is a charger that supports a speed of 140W, and the cable also supports up to 140W.  Let's start charging both devices from a completely discharged state .  The iPhone 17 Pro had 26% battery charge after 10 minutes and 51% after 20 minutes .  That's a whopping 51% increase in just 20 minutes.  That's exactly what Apple is talking about. And it also tells you that charging will be completed in 26 minutes up to 80% After 30 minutes it was fully charged to 68% and after 40 minutes it was fully charged to 78% .  And after about 43 minutes and 30 seconds, it was fully charged to 80%.  This time, the Galaxy Fold 7 had 18% battery charge after 10 minutes of charging, and 37% battery charge after 20 minutes .  And after 30 minutes, the battery was charged to 57%, and after 40 minutes, it was charged to 64% 43 minutes and 30 seconds, the time it took for 17% to reach 80% .  When charged under the same conditions, the final battery charge was 66%. This is a 14% difference from the iPhone .  Even though the Fold 7 has a slightly larger battery capacity, considering that the battery consumption is similar in real-world use, the conclusion is that the iPhone 17 Pro has much better battery efficiency .  I didn't stop there and continued charging until 80%, and it took a total of 57 minutes to fully charge to 80% .  Ultimately, I think the difference in physical capacity is very important, but when you consider actual battery consumption and battery charging speed during use, the iPhone 17's battery efficiency is far superior Design is a very personal matter. While many people think the Galaxy Fold 7's design is quite good, there are definitely those who prefer the iPhone 17 Pro's design .  The iPhone 17 Pro's body uses an aluminum unibody, and many people have expressed concerns about the use of aluminum, and these concerns are actually becoming reality .  This is because it is more vulnerable to breakage or damage when dropped, and scratches on the back compared to titanium .  But despite the aluminum body, Apple's finish is very clean.  When you touch it with your hand, the feel is even better than the previous generation.  In addition, the R value of the corners was applied significantly, so the grip was superior to the Galaxy Fold 7 when held in hand honestly, there's nothing to criticize about the Galaxy Fold 7's design Today, I've compiled a comparison of the iPhone 17 Pro and Galaxy Fold 7 after using them for a long time Ultimately, if you need a camera, high-performance performance, and Wiz, we recommend the iPhone 17. If you want to take advantage of a large display and the overall stability of Samsung Pay, we recommend the Fold 7.  So, please refer to my video and make reasonable purchases .  I've missed it so far.

title: iPhone 17/17 Pro/Pro Max: How to Turn Off Do not Disturb
transcript: Hey, what's up guys? So, in this video we will see how to turn off do not disturb mode on the iPhone 17, 17 Pro or the 17 Pro Max. Just swipe down from the top right of the screen and open the control center. And in the control center, you will find the option of do not disturb. Just tap on it, then tap on do not disturb mode to turn it off. Just go to your home screen and open settings. And in settings, you will find the option of focus. Just tap on it. Then tap on do not disturb. And from here just check if you have set any schedule for do not disturb mode or not. If you have set any schedule just tap on it then disable the schedule from here. Just like that. And this is how you can turn off do not disturb mode on the iPhone 17, 17 Pro, and the 17 Pro Max. Make sure to like, comment, and subscribe. Thank you for watching.
